intro: Sapranthus foetidus, commonly known as the stinking sapranthus, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Sapranthus foetidus include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sapranthus foetidus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential risks.
